findings.issue: Pre-launch blocker #15 (Open Badges 3.0 VC compliance) is marked as '🟥 ready to build (large)' with the description 'No DID issuer, no cryptographic proof generation, no revocation endpoint.' This is misleading. The file `apps/api/app/routers/badges.py` already implements the core Open Badges 3.0 Verifiable Credential JSON-LD structure, including the correct `@context` and an issuer endpoint. While a full W3C DID-based cryptographic proof and revocation system is indeed a large undertaking and not yet implemented, the foundational structure for Open Badges 3.0 VC is present.
findings.recommended_fix: Update `docs/PRE-LAUNCH-BLOCKERS-STATUS.md` for Blocker #15 to reflect the existing partial implementation of the Open Badges 3.0 Verifiable Credential JSON-LD structure in `apps/api/app/routers/badges.py`. Clarify that the remaining 'large' work involves implementing a full W3C DID-based cryptographic proof generation and a revocation endpoint, rather than implying no implementation exists at all.
